steps.title: Use the quadratic formula
steps.content:
For $3x^2-5x-5=0$, use $a=3$, $b=-5$, and $c=-5$ in the quadratic formula. This gives the two roots when the equation is not easy to factor.
$$x=\frac{-b\pm\sqrt{b^2-4ac}}{2a}$$

steps.title: Choose the greater solution
steps.content:
The greater solution uses the plus sign.
$$x=\frac{5+\sqrt{85}}{6}$$
The answer is B.
